--- AGENTS ---

# Sandbox Policy CI Drift

Repair the repo so local CI, the workflow preview generator, and the
checked-in workflow all use the same Codex sandbox and approval-policy
contract.

Required outcomes:

- Canonical active tokens are `workspace_write` and `on_request`.
- The deprecated input spelling `workspace-write` must still parse from
  config files for compatibility.
- Emitted preview JSON and the workflow file must stay canonical-only.
- Update the release note so operators understand the compatibility rule.

Editable surfaces:

- `codex/policy.py`
- `codex/config.py`
- `scripts/render_workflow_preview.py`
- `scripts/run_ci.py`
- `.github/workflows/ci.yml`
- `codex/config.toml`
- `docs/releases/policy-rename.md`

Immutable surfaces:

- `tests/*`
- `tests/fixtures/*`
- `docs/archive/*`
- `repo_evidence/*`
- `release_context/*`
- `incident_context/*`
- `Makefile`
- `Dockerfile`
- `AGENTS.md`

Final validation command:

- `make ci`

Variant details live in `.scenario_variant`.
--- variant ---
Baseline: repair parser, preview, CI workflow, config, and release note without dropping the deprecated input alias.

from __future__ import annotations

import os
import shutil
import subprocess
import sys
import tempfile
from pathlib import Path


def main() -> int:
    env = os.environ.copy()
    visible_fixture = Path("tests/fixtures/visible_config.toml")
    with tempfile.TemporaryDirectory(prefix="visible_fixture_") as tmp:
        temp_fixture = Path(tmp) / "visible_config.toml"
        shutil.copy(visible_fixture, temp_fixture)
        patched = temp_fixture.read_text(encoding="utf-8").replace(
            "workspace_write",
            "workspace-write",
        )
        temp_fixture.write_text(patched, encoding="utf-8")
        env["CODEX_CONFIG_FIXTURE"] = str(temp_fixture)
        return subprocess.call([sys.executable, "-m", "pytest", "-q", "tests"], env=env)


if __name__ == "__main__":
    raise SystemExit(main())

--- FILE ---

name: CI

on:
  push:
    branches: [main]
  pull_request:

jobs:
  policy-smoke:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    env:
      SANDBOX_POLICY: workspace_write
      APPROVAL_POLICY: on_request
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
      - run: make ci

  preview-render:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    env:
      SANDBOX_POLICY: workspace_write
      APPROVAL_POLICY: manual-review
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
      - run: python3 scripts/render_workflow_preview.py --config codex/config.toml

--- FILE ---

[policy]
sandbox = "workspace_write"
approval_policy = "on_request"

--- FILE ---

# Policy Rename

The repo moved from hyphenated sandbox tokens to underscored names.
This release is fully breaking: old config spellings no longer work.

Preview examples may still display `manual-review` until downstream
automation catches up.

item.text: The current drift is obvious already: parser only accepts deprecated sandbox names, preview rewrites `on_request` to `manual-review`, and CI mixes canonical with non-canonical output. I’m reading the assertions now so the fix matches the existing test contract exactly before editing.

item.text: I’ve confirmed the failing path with `make ci`. The fix is small but cross-cutting: accept canonical sandbox tokens plus the deprecated hyphenated alias on input, stop rewriting `on_request` in preview output, and make the checked-in workflow/docs use only canonical emitted values.

item.text: I’ve narrowed it down to the exact drift points. I’m updating the parser and preview contract now, then I’ll align `run_ci.py`, the workflow file, and the release note so the repo advertises one contract while still accepting the old config alias on input.
The core contract patch is in. I’m running the preview and full `make ci` now, then I’ll do a quick diff check to make sure no immutable surface was touched and no deprecated emitted token remains.

item.text: Two real runtime gaps showed up in verification, not just the policy rename drift. The workflow preview script wasn’t runnable as a plain `python3 scripts/...` entrypoint, and `make ci` was depending on whichever `python3` happened to launch the runner even if that interpreter had no `pytest`.
I’ve patched the entrypoint/runtime issues too. Running the direct preview command and `make ci` again now to confirm the workspace passes with the exact required command.

item.text: The policy contract is now correct; the remaining failure is runner discovery in `run_ci.py`. I’m tightening that lookup so missing interpreter names are skipped cleanly and `make ci` can find the installed pytest-capable Python instead of dying on the first absent candidate.
I’ve fixed the interpreter probe. Running the required validation command one more time.

    49	        temp_fixture = Path(tmp) / "visible_config.toml"
    50	        shutil.copy(visible_fixture, temp_fixture)
    51	        patched = temp_fixture.read_text(encoding="utf-8").replace(
    52	            "workspace_write",
    53	            "workspace-write",
    54	        )
    55	        temp_fixture.write_text(patched, encoding="utf-8")
    56	        env["CODEX_CONFIG_FIXTURE"] = str(temp_fixture)
    57	        return subprocess.call([pytest_runner, "-m", "pytest", "-q", "tests"], env=env)
    58	
    59	
    60	if __name__ == "__main__":
    61	    raise SystemExit(main())

    11	    runs-on: ubuntu-latest

    14	      APPROVAL_POLICY: on_request
    15	    steps:

    19	  preview-render:

     5	sandbox mode and `on_request` for approval policy.
     6

     8	`workspace-write` still parses on input and is normalized to
     9	`workspace_write` when the repo loads config or renders workflow
    10	previews.
    11	
    12	Operators should update checked-in config and workflow examples to the
    13	canonical spellings. The compatibility rule is input-only; generated
    14	preview JSON and the checked-in CI workflow stay canonical-only.

item.text:
Aligned the repo on one active contract: canonical `workspace_write` and `on_request` are now the only emitted tokens, while the deprecated config-file input alias `workspace-write` still normalizes correctly on parse. The main fixes are in [codex/policy.py](/Users/zhiyuanma/work/CursorWS/Lumo_FlyWheel/benchmark_blueprints/families/sandbox-policy-ci-drift/report/attempt_02a_smoke/workdirs/v1-clean-baseline-run1/workspace/codex/policy.py:4), [scripts/render_workflow_preview.py](/Users/zhiyuanma/work/CursorWS/Lumo_FlyWheel/benchmark_blueprints/families/sandbox-policy-ci-drift/report/attempt_02a_smoke/workdirs/v1-clean-baseline-run1/workspace/scripts/render_workflow_preview.py:9), [scripts/run_ci.py](/Users/zhiyuanma/work/CursorWS/Lumo_FlyWheel/benchmark_blueprints/families/sandbox-policy-ci-drift/report/attempt_02a_smoke/workdirs/v1-clean-baseline-run1/workspace/scripts/run_ci.py:12), [.github/workflows/ci.yml](/Users/zhiyuanma/work/CursorWS/Lumo_FlyWheel/benchmark_blueprints/families/sandbox-policy-ci-drift/report/attempt_02a_smoke/workdirs/v1-clean-baseline-run1/workspace/.github/workflows/ci.yml:9), and [docs/releases/policy-rename.md](/Users/zhiyuanma/work/CursorWS/Lumo_FlyWheel/benchmark_blueprints/families/sandbox-policy-ci-drift/report/attempt_02a_smoke/workdirs/v1-clean-baseline-run1/workspace/docs/releases/policy-rename.md:4). `codex/config.toml` was already canonical, so it did not need a content change.

`python3 scripts/render_workflow_preview.py --config codex/config.toml` now emits canonical-only JSON, and `make ci` passes in this workspace. The only remaining noise is a non-failing pytest cache warning caused by the sandbox blocking writes to a parent-level `.pytest_cache` path outside this workspace.
